title: While Loops
overview: While loops run as long as a condition is true. They're perfect when you don't know exactly how many times you need to repeat.

blocks.title: The while Loop
blocks.body: A `while` loop keeps running as long as its condition is True. It checks the condition BEFORE each iteration.
bullets: while condition: — checks before each run
If True, the body runs, then checks again

blocks.title: while is Like Checking Your Phone
blocks.text: While waiting for a friend: check for messages. No message → wait. Message arrives → stop. The while loop checks a condition and keeps going until it changes.

blocks.title: Basic while Loop
blocks.code:
count = 1
while count <= 5:
    print(f"Count: {count}")
    count = count + 1
print("Done!")
blocks.expectedOutput:
Count: 1
Count: 2
Count: 3
Count: 4
Count: 5
Done!

blocks.title: Avoiding Infinite Loops
blocks.body: An **infinite loop** runs forever because the condition never becomes False.
bullets: Always update the counter variable inside the loop
Make sure the condition will eventually become False
Use break to exit early if needed
Press Ctrl+C to stop a running program

blocks.question: What happens if you forget to update the counter inside a while loop?
blocks.answer: The condition never becomes False, creating an infinite loop. The program runs forever until you force-stop it with Ctrl+C.

blocks.title: Sum of N Numbers
blocks.instructions: Ask for a number N. Use a while loop to calculate sum of 1 to N.
blocks.starterCode:
n = int(input("Enter N: "))
count = 1
total = 0
while count <= n:
    total = 
    count = 
print(f"Sum: {total}")
blocks.solution:
n = int(input("Enter N: "))
count = 1
total = 0
while count <= n:
    total = total + count
    count = count + 1
print(f"Sum of 1 to {n} = {total}")
blocks.hint: Add count to total each iteration, then increment count.

blocks.title: Controlling Loop Flow
blocks.body: Sometimes you need to exit early or skip an iteration. break and continue give you this control.
bullets: continue — skips to the next iteration
break is useful for 'found it!' scenarios
continue is useful for 'skip this one' scenarios

blocks.title: break — Exit Early
blocks.expectedOutput:
Checking: 1
Checking: 2
Checking: 3
Checking: 4
Found 5! Stopping...

blocks.title: continue — Skip an Iteration
while vs for
blocks.body: Choose the right loop for the right job.
bullets: Use for when you know the count or have a list
Use while when waiting for a condition to change
for loops are safer (no infinite loops)

blocks.title: Number Guessing Game
blocks.instructions: Set secret = 7. Keep asking the user to guess until correct.
blocks.hint: Use while True for infinite loop, break when guessed correctly.
blocks.expectedOutput:
Guess (1-10): 3
Wrong, try again.
Guess (1-10): 7
Correct! You got it!

keyTakeaways.text: while loops run as long as the condition is True
Always update the condition variable to avoid infinite loops
continue skips to the next iteration
Use for when you know the count, while when you don't

whatYouLearned: How while loops work with conditions
How to use break and continue
When to use while vs for loops
How to build a number guessing game
